top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 87, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
Tour de Fukushima's rolling hills forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 87 miles.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Tour de Fukushima's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before June.
75ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
At Tour de Fukushima, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Tour de Fukushima. Train it until it feels boring.
6,562ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 87 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Tour de Fukushima, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
6,562ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
The aerobic base that lets you ride rolling hills at tempo for 87 miles without cracking. No shortcuts.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on rolling hills. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.

race_overlay.heat: June in Hamadori coastal region, Fukushima Prefecture can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before Tour de Fukushima. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Tour de Fukushima’s 87 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Tour de Fukushima. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Tour de Fukushima requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on rolling hills to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Gravel Specific, and Race Simulation to prepare you for 87 miles of rolling hills in Hamadori coastal region, Fukushima Prefecture.
